name: mcp-use
tagline: Fullstack framework to build, test, and deploy MCP servers and MCP apps
seoTitle: mcp-use: Fullstack MCP Server & App Framework
metaDescription: mcp-use is an open-source Python and TypeScript framework for building MCP servers and MCP apps for Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI agents, with a built-in inspector.
overview: mcp-use is a fullstack framework for the Model Context Protocol (MCP), the open standard that lets AI assistants like Claude and ChatGPT call external tools. With one SDK, available in both TypeScript and Python, you can define tools, build interactive widgets, and expose them to any MCP client.

Beyond writing servers, mcp-use ships a built-in Inspector to test and debug your tools, a CLI to deploy to production, and a Python agent SDK for connecting models to MCP tools. Templates and a starter command help you get a working server running in minutes.
features: Define MCP tools in TypeScript or Python with typed input schemas (Zod or Pydantic)
Build MCP Apps: React widgets that render inside Claude, ChatGPT, and other MCP clients
Auto-discovered widgets placed in a resources/ folder need no manual registration
Built-in Inspector to test and debug any MCP server interactively from the browser
CLI to log in and deploy servers to production, with logs, metrics, and branch deployments
Ready-to-use templates such as chart, diagram, slide deck, and map builders to remix

gettingStarted.intro: You can scaffold a new project, write a server by hand, or start with Python. The examples below come straight from the project README.

steps.heading: Scaffold a TypeScript project
steps.body: Use the starter command to create a ready-to-run MCP server or MCP app.

steps.heading: Define a tool and start the server
steps.body: Create a server, register a tool with a typed schema, and start listening. The Inspector is served automatically.

steps.heading: Or build a server in Python
steps.body: Install the package and define tools with Pydantic-annotated arguments, then run over streamable HTTP.

steps.heading: Deploy to production
steps.body: Log in and deploy your server with the mcp-use CLI, or connect a GitHub repo for managed hosting.

useCases: Expose your own APIs and data as MCP tools so AI agents like Claude can call them
Build interactive MCP App widgets that render once and run across Claude, ChatGPT, and other clients
Test and debug MCP servers locally or online with the built-in Inspector before shipping
Connect an AI model to MCP tools in Python using the agent SDK to automate tasks
